message:
type: SURVEY
files: tiktoken/core.py
note: Adding return_positions=True param to Encoding.encode() to return (tokens, positions) tuple tracking starting char offset of each token

type: SURVEY
files: tiktoken/core.py
note: Add Token Transformation: modify Encoding.encode() to accept new 'transformers' kwarg (single callable or list of callables applied sequentially to text before encoding)

type: CLAIM
files: tiktoken/core.py
note: I will own tiktoken/core.py since both our features touch encode(). Please send a SPEC for return_positions so I can implement it alongside transformers.

type: CLAIM
files: tiktoken/core.py
note: I'll own tiktoken/core.py and implement both features (return_positions and transformers) in encode(). Please send a SPEC with exact signature/behavior details for the transformers kwarg.

type: DEFER
files: tiktoken/core.py
spec: Add a new keyword-only parameter 'transformers' to Encoding.encode(self, text, *, allowed_special=set(), disallowed_special="all", transformers=None). Type: transformers: Callable[[str], str] | Sequence[Callable[[str], str]] | None = None. Behavior: if transformers is not None, before any special-token handling/regex matching, apply the transformation(s) to : if it's a single callable, call text = transformers(text); if it's a sequence of callables, apply them in order, each receiving the output of the previous one (text = f(text) for f in transformers). Each transformer must accept a str and return a str. This transformation must happen BEFORE the allowed_special/disallowed_special special-token-detection logic and before calling self._core_bpe.encode, so that transformed text (not the original) is what gets checked for disallowed special tokens and encoded. No changes needed to encode_ordinary, encode_to_numpy, or batch variants. Add a short docstring note and an example like: enc.encode("Hello World", transformers=str.lower) and enc.encode("Hello, World!", transformers=[str.lower, remove_punct]).
note: We both claimed core.py simultaneously (race) -- deferring to you as owner. Please implement transformers per this spec alongside your return_positions work in the same encode() method.

type: SPEC
files: tiktoken/core.py
spec: Add a new keyword-only parameter 'transformers' to Encoding.encode(self, text, *, allowed_special=set(), disallowed_special='all', transformers=None). Type: transformers: Callable[[str], str] | Sequence[Callable[[str], str]] | None = None. Behavior: if transformers is not None, before any special-token handling/regex matching, apply the transformation(s) to the text argument: if it is a single callable, do text = transformers(text); if it is a sequence of callables, apply them in order, each receiving the output of the previous one (i.e. text = f(text) for each f in transformers, sequentially). Each transformer must accept a str and return a str. This transformation must happen BEFORE the allowed_special/disallowed_special special-token-detection logic and before calling self dot underscore core_bpe dot encode, so that the transformed text (not the original) is what gets checked for disallowed special tokens and encoded. No changes needed to encode_ordinary, encode_to_numpy, or batch variants -- only the encode method. Add a short docstring note plus an example such as encode(hello world, transformers=str.lower) and encode with a list of two transformer functions applied in sequence.
note: NOTE: my previous message with this same content was corrupted by shell backtick expansion -- please use THIS message as the authoritative SPEC and disregard the garbled prior one. We both claimed core.py simultaneously; deferring ownership to you per your CLAIM.

type: DONE
files: tiktoken/core.py
note: Implemented both return_positions and transformers in Encoding.encode(). All existing tests pass (32/32). transformers applied before special-token check/regex/core_bpe.encode as specced; positions computed via decode_with_offsets on the resulting tokens.
